Espanola is under researched.  There is a stigma towards this community from Legislatures, Educated Groups, Outside States.  We are struggling.  We need Rehab Centers and initiatives to help the addicted/sick, youth, young, families and the elderly.  Espanola is becoming a 3rd World Country.  Please have some sympathy and/or empathy for the people here. Please work with Hector Balderas and our young mayor, who are the only ones who are willing to advocate for us here.  Also, "Espanola" is a broad home term that defines anyone who lives North of Santa Fe or in North Central New Mexico.

Rising costs of food are putting a strain on my budget even though I am not food or housing insecure. Rising costs of medical insurance are the same way. Although I have some extra money to spend on leisure, that percentage is going down significantly in recent months. My HH members and I have discussed getting chickens and growing food plants to help defray the costs of food.
Thank you for looking into this issue. It affects all members of out community directly and indirectly, even those like me who aren't currently experiencing food insecurity and housing uncertainty.

please consider that my responses do not represent the whole nor the majority of new mexico's population, I'm very blessed to me in my current position/household income but these types of issues are keeping me in an sometimes unhealthy relationship.

Thank you for doing this survey.
A salad bar somewhere besides La Posada (which is intended mainly for students) would be nice.
I believe there is a food waste issue on campus that could be used to help feed those who need.

I don't understand the point of this. You're not going to give me a raise. You're not going to help me with lunches at work. You just made me feel pretty miserable thinking about my situation that I try to put out of my head.

I have a good support system and am lucky enough to receive Medicaid, but if I did not have those I cannot imagine where I would be. Many people attending universities have a lot less. UNM has some programs to help their students, but they are still very expensive which is by far the biggest obstacle for students. I attended college there in my mid 20s so I had no scholarship even though I was a great student with great grades. I now have student loans I will be paying off for the next few decades. Making college classes less expensive, having cheaper parking passes, and better scholarships would go a long way to making housing and food more secure. SO much money went into my education and even more with interest will, too. Books, supplies, transportation, less working hours, and time away from home all cost money, too.

I feel like a lot of the services I'm being asked about are in person and currently being a remote employee, the service hours being during working hours as well as all the way on campus is frustrating and makes me feel like I'm being excluded in a way.
Thank you for doing this work. Many students I work with have shared stories of food and housing insecurity and it breaks my heart but I don't always know what to do.
I am worried that people have become so dependent on the govt subsidizing their lives that they realize there is no need to try and work hard to better themselves and their lives.
No thanks!
Several people I work with are working multiple jobs to make ends meet.  Even people with bachelor's degrees, working full time at UNM, are not making a living wage.

Biology has a food pantry because we found that students are hungry during the day, so we provide a place for them to grab a bite during the day or take home something for supper.  We are aware that hunger is an issue and we are trying to do a little to alleviate that situation for those who need it.

I think that UNM does a lot to improve the lives of many New Mexicans, either through education directly or through good jobs. I'm very happy here. I do think that there are quite a few people who work here (students, graduate students, various faculty positions) whose pay should be increased both for equity and their security.

Education is incredibly important for proper nutrition, but for people who donâ€™t have that kind of information it might be a bit more difficult to make those choices. So I think education should be just as much of a part of a pantry as the handing out of food.
Everything is connected. One aspect of food, housing, transportation, safety, etc, effects others, they are interconnected and when there is one inequity then there stems many inequities.

I know my responses put me in a "not food/housing-insecure" category, and I do have health insurance through my job, but I feel like one big health problem could shove me over into the "insecure" group.
I paid $19 for a salad at the UNM SUB's Mercado. The worst part is that there was no price tag on the salad.

Thank you for doing this survey/study and for trying to help those that do have housing/food insecurities!

I Having been a student in the bad old days some of the issues with food was that I did not qualify for any assistance,  My undergraduate was from Texas A&M and they had a great program from former students that supplied loans that did not have interest to pay tuition which could be paid off at the end of semester.  There were enough jobs around that I could work several and pay it off.  Graduated without debt.  Here there are not enough jobs even though the city is much bigger and federal loans is the only way, especially for graduate students, to make ends meet.  The grad students at Texas had health insurance which eased my burden.  Of course, the state had approved such a thing.  

One of my biggest peeves was from last years ice storm.  Students were stranded on campus and UNM did not do anything.  Calling school closed late and no preparations was very dangerous.  I was busing students to my daughters trailer since she lives on south campus.  CIty buses were stranded and I only found out later that the sun van has 4-wheel drive.  Still there were students that were stuck at the library and had no way home and the library was closing.  I would love to see a plan if students are trapped where they can shelter until the danger passes and they can get off campus.
I have a partner who makes a good income. If I didn't some of my answers may have been different.

The intersection of housing/food insecurity and intimate partner violence is very strong. People who are leaving abusive relationships may have high, accute, time sensitive needs, and we need interim as well as long term supports for them, and the current supports through the Women's Resource Center are wonderful but extremely understaffed and underfunded. They cannot support all of the need that exists.
Thank you for doing this survey and for looking for ways to assist those that are in need of food, housing and medical services.

I will hope that we always keep food and housing security as a primary goal in all of our planning. Students, staff and faculty cannot live full, inspiring lives without food and shelter security. Thank you for your work.

column6: Zoom events don't work. HR offers all of these stupid online programs that are a waste of energy. Don't reward people with shit that isn't good for the planet. Look at the problem with a wide angle lens instead of putting a band-aid on systematic issues. Giving people cheap food is less effective than exploring why they need it. This is the real problem; you'll never have a chance to get it right with a start like that.

Truly there is a massive housing problem in Silver City. Part of that is absolutely not on WNMU, but when employees cannot afford to get any of the expensive housing, it is an issue that they should care about for the wellbeing of their employees.

column1: What are your thoughts about food availability on your campus?
column6: it is expensive
I do not go to campus since my course is online
Good

I wish it were healthier.  I changed my diet a few years ago to low carb due to being diabetic.  I've also had a pork allergy that limits my selection.  I cannot find a selection that meets my new diet.
A little more would be nice
Let's hope it stays that way
The cafeteria doesnt serve hot food, there's only vending machines w/non-nutritious things.
scarce, but it works
junk food.  prepared poorly.  i always bring my own food.
Itâ€™s terrible

There are very limited options. Food for staff and students is an afterthought: we feel "lucky" to have food trucks or access to a food space that's mostly a launchpad for Street Food's other businesses.
It would be nice to have options on campus (other than Starbucks).  The food trucks are good when we have them, though not the lowest in cost.
Probably need more for the students
Limited selection and not always healthy, often lost of butter or oil.
only vending machines available
I wish it was more affordable
We have no food here n campus heathy or otherwise
CNM has no healthy food options on campus. Nutritious meals involve driving to the local co-op or a restaurant off-site.
Only one location and very minimal selection.
It is far from the building I work in and it's expensive to get a balanced, nutritious meal.
Cafeteria is located in a centralized location, Food Pantry is located in a easily accessible area.
Limited food choices on main campus, one or two food trucks and cafeteria.  Both options are expensive.
More options to choose would be nice.
It a good thing but I work at one of the satellite campuses and there is no where to purchase food.
too expensive from the food trucks, lack of other options around campus other than the bookstore

There is nowhere to buy food on satellite campuses except vending machines. 
Main campus does not, to my knowledge, have anywhere to buy lunch. At least we have starbucks. Please add mercados to CNM!

I donâ€™t know where the food is at CNM. At UNM, I use the DSH store because of the many jobs I work, so itâ€™s more convenient but there are not always many options and things are picked through. It is always expensive.
There is food available, maybe not foods for my diet but there is food available.
There is decent food options on campus. However, I have not purchased directly from the campus. Rather, I've bought from the food trucks stationed on campus.
On-campus food availability is located near student populated areas, which is good.
We have a small cafeteria open until 3pm.  We also have a Starbucks and a food truck.  That's about it.
CNM has done away with all the remote campus food services except vending machines. We are too far away from restaurants and need to bring food from home.
i only buy from cafeterias - wish have more food choices. It is understandable that it is based on # of visitors to the cafeteria
We need more options
It would be nice to have a choice when money allows
CNM organizes a food truck day once a week, but other than that, there aren't any other options other than Starbucks.
CNM relies on expensive food trucks and vending machines.
I try to bring my lunch; only junk food is affordable on campus
